pipeline_verified.cargo_doc_p_benten_ivm: clean (zero warnings, even with RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings)
pipeline_verified.cargo_nextest_p_benten_ivm: 49/53 pass; 4 failures confirmed as R3 test-design bugs (test builds a 'rebuilt' view that is never fed events, then asserts equality vs a populated incrementally-maintained view — see e.g. tests/view1_capability_grants.rs lines 74-96). Views' rebuild() is explicitly documented as state-reset, not event-replay (views/capability_grants.rs line 213-219, views/content_listing.rs line 125-134, views/version_current.rs line 204-211). Claim 3 in the brief is accurate.
pipeline_verified.test_files_touched_in_G5: none — git show --stat fca1ad6 fe52264 6e0ddaa shows only src/ changes; no tests/ paths. Confirms TDD contract held.

findings.claim: #[path]-based inline `pub mod views { ... }` composition is unconventional but acceptable for Phase 1.
findings.detail: The chicken-and-egg G5-B/G5-C parallel-agent coordination is explained in the comment block at lines 48-56. The scheme correctly composes five files under a single parent module without any of the three G5 agents touching each other's files. Cost: (a) cargo doc renders the files at views::capability_grants etc., which is fine; (b) newcomers will expect views/mod.rs and pause at the `#[path]` attribute. Benefit of normalizing now: ~10 lines of churn (delete #[path], create views/mod.rs with the pub use statements, move the docstring). This is worth doing as a tidying pass before G8 — not blocking G5 acceptance. Rustdoc output verified clean; discoverability is uncompromised because `views::<name>` paths resolve correctly.
findings.suggested_fix: Defer to a tidying commit. When done: create `crates/benten-ivm/src/views/mod.rs` with the module docstring and `pub use` re-exports currently inlined in lib.rs; remove the `#[path = "..."]` attributes from lib.rs; shrink `pub mod views` in lib.rs to a single `pub mod views;` line. No semantic change.

findings.claim: The 'two paths per view' pattern (update(&ChangeEvent) vs on_change(Node)) is a real maintenance burden but is currently acceptable and well-documented.
findings.detail: Each view maintains semantically divergent behavior depending on which ingress path is used: the ChangeEvent path falls back to tx_id (view 3) or hardcoded anchor_id=1 (view 5), while on_change(Node) uses real createdAt / real anchor_id. Both paths count against budget differently in some views. The divergence is a direct consequence of the G3 ChangeEvent shape; it is plainly flagged as a Phase-1 compromise (content_listing.rs lines 18-25; version_current.rs lines 9-23). The risk is that a future reader fixes one path and forgets the other, silently introducing behavior drift. The mitigation already in place (comments labeling the compromise and linking to Phase 2) is the right one for Phase 1; once ChangeEvent widens, the two paths should converge on a single implementation.
findings.suggested_fix: When ChangeEvent grows property-carrying shape in Phase 2, fold on_change(Node) into update(&ChangeEvent) as the single ingress; keep on_change only if there is a direct test harness use-case. Track in the named-compromise ledger (CLAUDE.md compromise #N) so this isn't forgotten.

findings.claim: rebuild() restoring budget to u64::MAX is undocumented at the View-trait site.
findings.detail: Views 3 and 5 restore the original budget to u64::MAX on rebuild() so a stale view can accept fresh events. View 1 (capability_grants.rs:212-220) and View 2 (event_handler_dispatch.rs:165-169) do NOT restore budget in rebuild(); a stale view 1 that gets rebuilt will immediately trip stale again on the next event. View 4 (governance_inheritance.rs:257-261) also does not restore budget. The rebuild-behavior inconsistency between views is real but not caught by any current test; the proptest-based equivalence tests that WOULD catch it are the ones flagged as R3 test-design bugs.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) standardize all five views to restore budget on rebuild() (matches the 'a Fresh view should work' expectation implied by the ViewState docs), or (b) document the per-view budget-on-rebuild policy on the View::rebuild trait doc. Option (a) is the lower-risk fix and matches the user-visible claim in views 3/5 already. This is a Phase-1 tightening task, not a G5 blocker.

findings.claim: The `#[allow(clippy::print_stderr)]` for eprintln! is scoped correctly and acceptable Phase 1.
findings.detail: The allow is function-scoped (not crate-scoped) and carries a `reason = "..."` string naming Phase 1 + the Phase 2 migration target (tracing). The sink is only reachable for non-fatal, non-budget ViewError variants — PatternMismatch is the practical case — which are a caller-side 'this view doesn't handle this query' signal rather than an operational alert. The TODO(phase-2) comment at line 171-172 names both the sink swap and the semantic intent clearly. Acceptable.
findings.suggested_fix: No change for Phase 1. When tracing lands in Phase 2, replace the eprintln! with `tracing::warn!(view_id, error = %err, "...")` and remove the allow.

findings.claim: Doc-comment coverage is inconsistent across views.
findings.detail: The workspace Cargo.toml does not enforce `missing_docs`, so this is not a clippy failure. However, 5 of the pub items in event_handler_dispatch.rs (new, definition, with_budget_for_testing, and 3 of EffectiveRules' accessors — governance_inheritance.rs depth()/was_truncated()/cycle_detected()/rules()) are missing doc comments; version_current.rs new/definition/with_budget_for_testing/state likewise lack docs. Views 3 (content_listing) and 1 (capability_grants) have consistently high doc coverage; views 2/4/5 are patchier. This asymmetry is purely cosmetic but it's noticeable when reading through — a single `///` pass would close it.
findings.suggested_fix: One-pass: add one-line doc comments to the undocumented pub items in event_handler_dispatch.rs, governance_inheritance.rs, and version_current.rs. Mirror the docstring style used in capability_grants.rs and content_listing.rs. ~15 lines of additions, zero behavior change.

findings.claim: Read-side API surface drift across the 5 views.
findings.detail: All 5 views implement View::update(&ChangeEvent) -> Result<(), ViewError> consistently. BudgetExceeded is handled uniformly (flips state + returns Err). However the *direct* (non-trait) read methods diverge: View 1 has `read_for_entity(&Cid) -> Result<Vec<Cid>, ViewError>`; View 2 has `read_handlers_for_event(&str) -> Result<Vec<Cid>, ViewError>`; View 3 has both `read_page(usize, usize) -> Result<Vec<Cid>, ViewError>` and `read_page_allow_stale(usize, usize) -> Result<Vec<Cid>, ViewError>`; View 4 has `effective_rules(&Cid) -> EffectiveRules` (infallible) AND `read_effective_rules(&Cid) -> Result<EffectiveRules, ViewError>` (fallible); View 5 has `resolve<A: AnchorRef>(A) -> Result<Option<Cid>, ViewError>`. Only View 3 exposes a relaxed-read counterpart (allow_stale); Views 1/2/4/5 do not have an `_allow_stale` variant. The trait-side View::read(&ViewQuery) is the consistent shape across all 5 and is what the G7 engine orchestrator + G8 TS wrapper will consume, so the drift is only on per-view convenience methods. Still: G7 and G8 agents will have to squint at five different signatures, and test authors will too.
findings.suggested_fix: (Non-blocking) Pick one of two paths: (a) remove direct-read methods and route all reads through View::read(&ViewQuery), accepting that the query is slightly awkward; or (b) standardize direct-read naming and signature: `fn read_<shape>(&self, key) -> Result<T, ViewError>` plus optional `fn read_<shape>_allow_stale(&self, key) -> Result<T, ViewError>`, where `<shape>` is the same noun used on the View::read ViewQuery field. Path (b) preserves the ergonomic direct-read API while eliminating the 5-way drift.

findings.claim: Cascade deletion / cleanup semantics differ across views; one is arguably leaky.
findings.detail: View 3 (content_listing): Deleted events remove ALL entries matching the CID — correct, no leak. View 1 (capability_grants): Deleted iterates every entity's grant set and removes the CID, then drops empty sets — correct, no leak. View 5 (version_current): Deleted only removes the DEFAULT_ANCHOR_ID entry (line 188); any entries stored under other anchor_ids via on_change(Node) are left in place. Under the Phase-1 single-anchor convention this is fine (there IS only anchor_id=1 via the trait path). View 4 (governance_inheritance): the apply_event path explicitly does nothing (line 167-181, 'identity-only events are acknowledged without state mutation'). The parent map is populated ONLY via add_edge, so a Deleted event for a GovernedBy edge does NOT remove the adjacency — the view leaks edges that the graph has already deleted. In Phase 1 tests this is not exercised because add_edge is the sole edge ingress.
findings.suggested_fix: View 4: when a Deleted event for a GovernedBy-labeled node arrives, at minimum remove any parent mapping whose value equals event.cid (best-effort cleanup). Or document the leak explicitly (in addition to the ChangeEvent-shape note) since the lag between graph-delete and view-cleanup is a maintenance-visibility issue. View 5: if Phase-2 ChangeEvent will carry anchor_id, consider deferring the cleanup fix to that point; meanwhile add a comment saying the Deleted path only unseats DEFAULT_ANCHOR_ID.

findings.claim: View trait object-safety is correctly assertion-tested and the trait is object-safe.
findings.detail: Line 320-323 installs `fn _assert_view_object_safe() { fn _takes(_: Box<dyn View>) {} }` as a compile-time assertion. Trait methods are all dyn-compatible (no generic methods, no Self: Sized except the default mark_stale which is fine). Subscriber holds `Vec<Box<dyn View>>` (subscriber.rs:54) and the workspace compiles, confirming at-runtime as well. No issue; flagging as a positive finding for the mini-review record.

findings.claim: Send + Sync bounds correctly declared on the View trait; Subscriber's Mutex<Vec<Box<dyn View>>> is sound.
findings.detail: `pub trait View: Send + Sync` at view.rs:206 forces every impl to be Send+Sync. All 5 view structs contain only BTreeMap/BTreeSet/u64/bool/Cid/String — all Send+Sync. Subscriber wraps the Vec in std::sync::Mutex so the `&self`-taking ChangeSubscriber::on_change can mutate views; on poison it falls through to into_inner which is correct for a non-UB-producing panic recovery path. No gap.

findings.claim: View 3 (exit criterion): pagination cursor type is offset-based, not an opaque cursor. Acceptable for Phase 1 but will not match what G8 + the `crud('post').list` DSL conventionally expects.
findings.detail: The public `read_page(offset: usize, limit: usize)` and `ViewQuery.offset/limit` produce a standard LIMIT/OFFSET pagination. Most modern APIs (and what QUICKSTART.md's `crud('post').list` likely wants) use cursor-based pagination keyed on the sort field (here: createdAt). Offset-based pagination drifts under concurrent inserts — an acceptable Phase-1 trade. The composite SortKey = (i64, u64) exposed at line 50 is the right internal shape for a cursor later; encoding that as `Cursor(Option<(createdAt, disambiguator)>)` would take the same BTreeMap range but be stable under inserts. The tx_id-as-createdAt-fallback for the ChangeEvent path (line 274) IS clearly documented as a Phase-1 compromise at lines 18-25 and inline at 271-273 — finding g5-cr-2 covers the broader pattern.
findings.suggested_fix: For G8: the TS wrapper should expose offset/limit on the ContentListingView surface (matches current API), with a TODO(phase-2) to swap to cursor-based once the sort-key story stabilizes. Phase-2: add `read_page_from(cursor: Option<SortKey>, limit: usize) -> (Vec<Cid>, Option<SortKey>)` that does a range scan instead of skip().

findings.claim: ViewError::PatternMismatch maps to ErrorCode::InputLimit, which is a semantic mismatch.
findings.detail: The inline comment at line 74-75 says 'PatternMismatch is a caller-side input issue; catalog code E_INPUT_LIMIT is the closest match in the current catalog.' But InputLimit connotes 'you sent too much' (a size/budget failure) whereas PatternMismatch means 'your query shape does not name any index this view maintains' — closer to E_INVALID_ARG or E_VIEW_PATTERN_MISMATCH. The current mapping will confuse cross-language consumers when they surface the code to users. Check ERROR-CATALOG.md: if there's a more appropriate code (e.g., InputInvalid, QueryShapeInvalid), use that; otherwise add one.
findings.suggested_fix: Consult docs/ERROR-CATALOG.md; if no better code exists, add E_IVM_PATTERN_MISMATCH to the catalog and map PatternMismatch → that. If the catalog is frozen for Phase 1, swap to whatever input-validation code IS present (likely InputInvalid, not InputLimit). Document the chosen code in ViewError::code doc comment.

findings.claim: route_change_event (pub API) and on_change (ChangeSubscriber impl) duplicate the fan-out loop body.
findings.detail: Both methods take the mutex guard, iterate views, and call apply_event. route_change_event returns Ok(applied) while on_change discards the count. The duplication is minor (6 lines) and is functionally equivalent. A small cleanup would factor the loop into `fn fan_out(&self) -> usize` that both call. Not a blocker.
findings.suggested_fix: Optional: extract the shared body to `fn fan_out(&self, event: &ChangeEvent) -> usize { ... }` private method. Both callers reduce to one line.

findings.claim: Back-compat type aliases (CapGrants, EventHandlerDispatchView) are carrying test-ergonomic baggage into the public API.
findings.detail: Both views export aliases (`pub type CapGrants = CapabilityGrantsView;`, `pub type EventHandlerDispatchView = EventDispatchView;`) to satisfy R3 tests that used one naming while G5-B used another. These are now public items. Long-term this doubles the surface without adding value — deprecation is a Phase-2 concern. For now they are documented as 'back-compat alias / some tests use this name', which is honest.
findings.suggested_fix: Once R3 tests have a cleanup pass (R4b or later), rename test usages to the canonical type names and remove the aliases. Don't ship aliases into the TS bindings.

findings.detail: After G5 the crate is no longer a stub — it ships 5 hand-written views + subscriber + content-addressed ViewDefinition. The 'stub' wording in the description will confuse anyone reading crates.io-style metadata. Similarly `STUB_MARKER` at lib.rs:46 is still present.
findings.suggested_fix: Update description to 'Incremental View Maintenance for the Benten graph engine — Phase 1 hand-written views (capability grants, event dispatch, content listing, governance inheritance, version current).' Keep STUB_MARKER if the R3 tests assert on it; otherwise rename to PHASE_MARKER or remove.

self_flagged_item_verdicts.item_1_path_attribute: Accepted as Phase-1 pattern. See g5-cr-1 for recommended cleanup at tidying time, non-blocking.
self_flagged_item_verdicts.item_2_two_paths_per_view: Real maintenance burden but correctly documented. See g5-cr-2. Acceptable Phase 1 pending ChangeEvent widening.
self_flagged_item_verdicts.item_3_rebuild_state_reset: Confirmed by reading the four failing tests. Views' rebuild() contract is state-reset (and the docs say so); the test assertion is miswired. This is a test-authoring bug, not a view-impl bug. Non-blocking for G5; goes into the R4b review backlog.
self_flagged_item_verdicts.item_4_eprintln_allow: Accepted. Allow is function-scoped with reason string; log sink is named; Phase-2 migration to tracing is TODO-tagged. See g5-cr-4.

generic_audit_results.module_boundaries: Sensible. view.rs = types, subscriber.rs = fan-out, views/*.rs = impls. No circular imports (views depend on crate::View trait; subscriber depends on crate::view types; no reverse edge).
generic_audit_results.viewdefinition_system_label_leak: Not a leak. 'system:IVMView' is a system-zone label; the graph write path rejects unprivileged writes with E_SYSTEM_ZONE_WRITE (crates/benten-graph/src/redb_backend.rs:56-62). View definitions written via the privileged engine path are correctly content-addressed — that is the design intent.
generic_audit_results.unwrap_expect_in_view_impls: None in non-test code paths. All unwrap_or_default / unwrap_or / unwrap_or_else(PoisonError::into_inner) are total / infallibility-expressing. Test code uses .unwrap() which is acceptable.
generic_audit_results.error_messages_stable: Yes. VIEW_ID consts ensure the view name in Stale{view_id:...} is stable across rebuilds and refactors.
generic_audit_results.send_sync_bounds: All 5 views satisfy Send+Sync automatically. See g5-cr-9.
generic_audit_results.object_safety: Compile-time assertion at view.rs:320-323 plus runtime use in Subscriber confirms. See g5-cr-8.
generic_audit_results.api_consistency_across_5_views: Trait side: consistent. Direct-read methods: see g5-cr-6 for drift.
generic_audit_results.test_file_changes: None in G5 commits (git show --stat confirmed). TDD contract upheld.
generic_audit_results.cargo_toml_deps: benten-core + benten-graph + thiserror + dev-deps (tempfile, proptest, criterion). Minimal and appropriate. proptest correctly gated to dev-dependencies.
generic_audit_results.cargo_doc_warnings: Zero (verified with RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc -p benten-ivm --no-deps).
generic_audit_results.coordination_artifacts: One TODO(phase-2) in subscriber.rs:171 (acknowledged, named, scoped). No leftover merge-conflict markers. The G5-B/G5-C coordination explainer at lib.rs:48-56 is an informational comment, not technical debt.

notes:
Overall verdict: accept-with-minors. Pipeline is green on the structural checks (fmt, clippy -D warnings, cargo doc -D warnings); nextest shows 49/53 passing with the 4 remaining failures verified as R3 test-authoring bugs (the tests compare an empty-rebuilt view against a populated-incremental view; the views' rebuild() is documented state-reset, not event-replay). The G5 implementation is honest, well-documented, and preserves a clean trait-object-safe contract that G7 + G8 can consume.

None of the 14 findings block G5 exit. The highest-leverage follow-ups, roughly in priority order:
  1. g5-cr-3 (rebuild() budget-restore consistency across views 1/2/4) — should land before G7 so engine-level rebuild callers have uniform semantics
  2. g5-cr-6 (direct-read method drift) — should land before G8 so the TS wrapper isn't navigating 5 inconsistent shapes
  3. g5-cr-7 (View 4 governance cleanup on Deleted) — document or fix before G7
  4. g5-cr-11 (PatternMismatch error-code mapping) — quick catalog lookup, low effort
  5. g5-cr-14 (Cargo.toml 'stub' description) — cosmetic, trivial

Items 1/2/7/8/9/10/12/13 are tidying-pass material — collect into a G5-followup commit after G6 lands or include in the R4b post-impl review. The `#[path]` coordination pattern (g5-cr-1) can ship as-is.
